title: podman push command uploads the image every time again even if it already exists
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT**

/kind bug


**"podman push" command uploads the image every time again, even if it already exists**

Problem is reprodused on `podman` 2.0.1-3.1.2
It is reprodused with all versions of `nexus` and `artifactory`.
But it isn't reprodesed on `docker.io`
It is reproduced only if podman cache is empty (/var/lib/containers/storage/cache/blob-info-cache-v1.boltdb).
`docker push` does not reproduce this error.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. pull image
`podman push docker.io/library/centos:7`

2. tag image for local nexus/artifactory
`podman tag docker.io/library/centos:7 10.10.10.10:17001/library/centos:7`

3. push it first time to local-network registry
`podman push 10.10.10.10:17001/library/centos:7 --tls-verify=false`

4. remove all images
`podman rmi -a -f`

5. pull image again and tag it
```
podman push docker.io/library/centos:7
podman tag docker.io/library/centos:7 10.10.10.10:17001/library/centos:7
```

6. try to upload image again - works correctly, doesn't upload all layers again - all layer already exist
`podman push 10.10.10.10:17001/library/centos:7 --tls-verify=false`

7. delete storage folder of podman
`rm -rf /var/lib/containers/storage`

8. pull image again and tag it
```
podman push docker.io/library/centos:7
podman tag docker.io/library/centos:7 10.10.10.10:17001/library/centos:7
```

9. **ERROR!!!** try to upload image again - works incorrectly, podman upload all layers again
`podman push 10.10.10.10:17001/library/centos:7 --tls-verify=false`


**Additional information**
If you delete cache file than podman again uploading existing layers
```
[root@ff4eb9197bdd /]# podman push 10.10.10.10:17001/library/centos-third:7 --tls-verify=false
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 174f56854903 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 8652b9f0cb [======================================] 2.7KiB / 2.7KiB
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[root@ff4eb9197bdd /]# podman tag docker.io/library/centos:7 10.10.10.10:17001/library/centos-third:7
[root@ff4eb9197bdd /]# rm -f /var/lib/containers/storage/cache/blob-info-cache-v1.boltdb
[root@ff4eb9197bdd /]# podman pull docker.io/library/centos:7
Trying to pull docker.io/library/centos:7...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 2d473b07cdd5 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 8652b9f0cb done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
8652b9f0cb4c0599575e5a003f5906876e10c1ceb2ab9fe1786712dac14a50cf
[root@ff4eb9197bdd /]# podman push 10.10.10.10:17001/library/centos-third:7 --tls-verify=false
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 174f56854903 [=>------------------------------------] 12.0MiB / 201.9MiB
Copying blob 174f56854903 done
Copying config 8652b9f0cb [======================================] 2.7KiB / 2.7KiB
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

comments.body: Please try with a more recent version of Podman and fill out the data requested in the issue template.  Knowing the host environment is crucial to resolve issues in many cases.

Hi! Any news? Or should I add some additional information?

> Hi! Any news? Or should I add some additional information?

Sorry for the silence. We are very busy at the moment but will look into the issue.

I finally managed to get back to this issue and read all the logs.  First, thanks for providing the logs; they are super helpful.

>DEBU[0001] HEAD http://10.10.10.10:17001/v2/library/centos-third/blobs/sha256:174f5685490326fc0a1c0f5570b8663732189b327007e47ff13d2ca59673db02
> DEBU[0001] ... not present

That means that the registry claims that the blob is not present, so Podman will upload it and create a record in the local cache that it has been uploaded.  Hence, Podman will push again once the cache has been deleted.

Maybe the registry misses a specific configuration option?
I am going to close the issue but we can continue the conversation here.

Hello i just start local nexus and done following steps

1) podman pull hello-world --log-level=debug
2) podman tag hello-world nexu/hw:latest
3) podman push nexus/hw:latest --log-level=debug
4) podman push nexus/hw:latest --log-level=debug

During pull we got blob
```
DEBU[0003] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/hello-world/blobs/sha256:b8dfde127a2919ff59ad3fd4a0776de178a555a76fff77a506e128aea3ed41e3

```
Manifest https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/hello-world/manifests/sha256:1b26826f602946860c279fce658f31050cff2c596583af237d971f4629b57792
```
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
  "config": {
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.container.image.v1+json",
    "size": 1470,
    "digest": "sha256:d1165f2212346b2bab48cb01c1e39ee8ad1be46b87873d9ca7a4e434980a7726"
  },

    {

      "size": 2529,
      "digest": "sha256:b8dfde127a2919ff59ad3fd4a0776de178a555a76fff77a506e128aea3ed41e3"
    }
  ]
}
```

During 1st push
```
DEBU[0001] HEAD https://cloud-nexus.netcracker.com:20001/v2/hw/blobs/sha256:f22b99068db93900abe17f7f5e09ec775c2826ecfe9db961fea68293744144bd
DEBU[0002] ... not present
```

Generated manifest
```
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
  "config": {
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.container.image.v1+json",
    "size": 1470,
    "digest": "sha256:d1165f2212346b2bab48cb01c1e39ee8ad1be46b87873d9ca7a4e434980a7726"
  },
  "layers": [
    {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.image.rootfs.diff.tar.gzip",
      "size": 2607,
      "digest": "sha256:a99912efe9c767b280c869d2e734bef3c92d29d45d4e3beefbeb5a1924ed7445"
    }
  ]
}
```

During second push

```
DEBU[0002] HEAD https://cloud-nexus.netcracker.com:20001/v2/hw/blobs/sha256:a99912efe9c767b280c869d2e734bef3c92d29d45d4e3beefbeb5a1924ed7445
DEBU[0003] ... already exists
DEBU[0003] Skipping blob sha256:f22b99068db93900abe17f7f5e09ec775c2826ecfe9db961fea68293744144bd (already present):
```

As i could see we have 3 Blob SHA256:

1st  **sha256:b8dfde127a2919ff59ad3fd4a0776de178a555a76fff77a506e128aea3ed41e3** downloaded from docker.io and mentioned in original manifest

2nd **sha256:f22b99068db93900abe17f7f5e09ec775c2826ecfe9db961fea68293744144bd** calculated by podman and used during 1st upload

3rd **sha256:a99912efe9c767b280c869d2e734bef3c92d29d45d4e3beefbeb5a1924ed7445** generated during upload and placed in nexus/hw:latest manifest also used by podman to check if layer exist.

Log during push show us that podman do blob sha changes: 
```
DEBU[0000] Checking /v2/hw/blobs/sha256:f22b99068db93900abe17f7f5e09ec775c2826ecfe9db961fea68293744144bd
DEBU[0000] GET https://cloud-nexus.netcracker.com:20001/v2/
DEBU[0000] Ping https://cloud-nexus.netcracker.com:20001/v2/ status 401
DEBU[0000] GET https://cloud-nexus.netcracker.com:20001/v2/token?account=admin&scope=repository%3Ahw%3Apull%2Cpush&service=https%3A%2F%2Fcloud-nexus.netcracker.com%3A20001%2Fv2%2Ftoken
DEBU[0001] Increasing token expiration to: 60 seconds
DEBU[0001] HEAD https://cloud-nexus.netcracker.com:20001/v2/hw/blobs/sha256:f22b99068db93900abe17f7f5e09ec775c2826ecfe9db961fea68293744144bd
DEBU[0002] ... not present
DEBU[0002] exporting filesystem layer "f22b99068db93900abe17f7f5e09ec775c2826ecfe9db961fea68293744144bd" without compression for blob "sha256:f22b99068db93900abe17f7f5e09ec775c2826ecfe9db961fea68293744144bd"
DEBU[0002] No compression detected
DEBU[0002] Compressing blob on the fly
DEBU[0002] Uploading /v2/hw/blobs/uploads/
DEBU[0002] POST https://cloud-nexus.netcracker.com:20001/v2/hw/blobs/uploads/
Copying blob f22b99068db9 [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 15.0KiB
Copying blob f22b99068db9 done
Copying blob f22b99068db9 done
DEBU[0004] Upload of layer sha256:a99912efe9c767b280c869d2e734bef3c92d29d45d4e3beefbeb5a1924ed7445 complete
```

I do the same several times
1) pull
2) tag
3) push
4) rm -rf /var/lib/containers/*

and each time have following transformation
During pull
```
b8dfde127a2919ff59ad3fd4a0776de178a555a76fff77a506e128aea3ed41e3 -> f22b99068db93900abe17f7f5e09ec775c2826ecfe9db961fea68293744144bd
```

During push
```
f22b99068db93900abe17f7f5e09ec775c2826ecfe9db961fea68293744144bd -> a99912efe9c767b280c869d2e734bef3c92d29d45d4e3beefbeb5a1924ed7445
```

So it look like a bug that podman push **a99912efe9c767b280c869d2e734bef3c92d29d45d4e3beefbeb5a1924ed7445** but check for **f22b99068db93900abe17f7f5e09ec775c2826ecfe9db961fea68293744144bd**

By the way i done one more test with push and pull to docker hub

```
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ status 401
DEBU[0000] GET https://auth.docker.io/token?account=bulanovk&scope=repository%3Abulanovk%2Fhw%3Apull%2Cpush&service=registry.docker.io
DEBU[0001] HEAD https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/bulanovk/hw/blobs/sha256:f22b99068db93900abe17f7f5e09ec775c2826ecfe9db961fea68293744144bd
DEBU[0001] ... not present
DEBU[0001] Trying to reuse cached location sha256:b8dfde127a2919ff59ad3fd4a0776de178a555a76fff77a506e128aea3ed41e3 compressed with gzip in docker.io/library/hello-world
DEBU[0001] Checking /v2/library/hello-world/blobs/sha256:b8dfde127a2919ff59ad3fd4a0776de178a555a76fff77a506e128aea3ed41e3
DEBU[0001] GET https://auth.docker.io/token?account=bulanovk&scope=repository%3Abulanovk%2Fhw%3Apull%2Cpush&scope=repository%3Alibrary%2Fhello-world%3Apull&service=registry.docker.io
DEBU[0002] HEAD https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/hello-world/blobs/sha256:b8dfde127a2919ff59ad3fd4a0776de178a555a76fff77a506e128aea3ed41e3
DEBU[0003] ... already exists
```

And according to the log, podman try to find blob by Original ID in case of docker hub.

The change of IDs is to be expected since the layers are stored _uncompressed_.  Hence, when pushing we see the digests of the layers in uncompressed form.  That's where the cache comes into play, which allows for mapping the layers in their uncompressed and compressed form.
Is i understand you correctly, podman pushing compressed blob but looking it by uncompressed hash?

> Is i understand you correctly, podman pushing compressed blob but looking it by uncompressed hash?

Yes, Podman is first checking if the layer exists in uncompressed form on the registry. Then it will check if the layer exists in any of the recorded compressed forms and that requires the blob-info cache.  Hence, if the cache is removed, the checks won't hit.

I'm sorry to reopen this case but I have the problem too.

Is there a way to compress on the fly, then hash and check the against the repository before sending the blob ?
@guillomep please open a new issue with the exact issue and expected behavior.  I am sure we'll find a solution.
